version_date: Official Version as at 19 Feb 2026 (consolidation PCO 04-af0-00)
scope_summary: The principal planning statute for Western Australia. It establishes the Western Australian Planning Commission, and provides for State planning policies (Part 3), planning codes (Part 3A, under which the R-Codes are made - s 291 deems the R-Codes to be a planning code), region and local planning schemes, interim development orders, planning control areas, improvement plans and schemes, subdivision control, development assessment (including DAP-related provisions), compensation, and enforcement. It applies state-wide and is the head of power for nearly every other planning instrument in the corpus.
definitions: Swan Valley / Swan Valley Planning Scheme (s 4(1))

cross_references.cited: Metropolitan Region Town Planning Scheme Act 1959
